response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and embedded software engineer with a solid background in TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web/mobile systems, but has no meaningful alignment with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization at the silicon level. The candidate's AI exposure is limited to MCP tooling and agentic workflow infrastructure — application-layer work, not accelerator hardware. Their low-level work (NI LabWindows C/C++, DLang async I/O) is software performance engineering, not hardware performance modeling. There is no evidence of SIMD/MIMD architecture knowledge, DNN inference optimization, or cloud infrastructure hardware experience.
strengths: Long career (15+ years) with demonstrated ability to work across the stack, including low-level C/C++ and DLang for performance-sensitive systems
Experience writing low-latency software with async I/O, fibers, and caching — shows some performance-oriented mindset
Exposure to embedded and hardware-adjacent domains (robotic arms, NI LabWindows instrumentation software)
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware performance analysis — the core technical requirements of this role
AI experience is limited to application-layer agent tooling (MCP, agentic workflows), with no background in deep neural network training, inference optimization, or AI accelerator hardware
Educational background (BS in CS + BS in Photography) combined with a software-focused career does not meet the seniority threshold for a principal-level hardware architecture role

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or GPU architecture experience
